title: Ship Rotation, Thrust, Inertia, Drag, and Speed Limits · Transfer Build
summary: An independent adaptive-arcade team receives a different viewport, device, frame rate, seed, entity count, accessibility profile, platform, and delivery constraint and transfers ship rotation, thrust, inertia, drag, and speed limits into a changed frame-rate-independent inertial ship controller. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of frame-rate-independent inertial ship controller with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit t…
objectives: Update angle from action intent and derive a normalized facing vector without overwriting velocity direction.
Integrate acceleration into velocity and velocity into position with explicit seconds and bounded time.
Preserve momentum and apply a time-scaled damping model whose behavior is tested across frame rates.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Ship Facing
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Asteroids team handles case 2145 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to integrate rotation, thrust, inertia, drag, and speed limits with frame-rate-independent Vector2 motion; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Ship Facing.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for update angle from action intent and derive a normalized facing vector without overwriting velocity direction. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Ship Facing, keep Ship Facing connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Ship Feel Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-ship-feel-evidence-tx92ll" that uses case 6982 to integrate rotation, thrust, inertia, drag, and speed limits with frame-rate-independent Vector2 motion. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Ship Feel Evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Ship Feel Evidence into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Ship Feel Evidence, keep Ship Feel Evidence connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-ship-feel-evidence-tx92ll
# Competency: asteroids-ship-feel-evidence.
# Case 6982: a keyboard-only community arcade night.
# Operating constraint: preserve explicit pixels, seconds, radians or degrees, frames, entity counts, collision units, and budgets.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def evidence_asteroids_ship_feel_evidence_tx92ll(speed: float = 140.0, thrust: float = 80.0, drag: float = 0.15, max_speed: float = 260.0, dt: float = 0.016) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_tx92ll = "tx92ll-ll29xt-6982"
    assert evidence_variant_tx92ll
    evidence_axes_tx92ll = (
        "playertx92ll",
        "seed6982",
        "inputll29xt",
        "frametx92ll6982",
        "state6982tx92ll",
        "playtestll29xt6982",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_tx92ll)) == 6
    if min(speed, thrust, drag, max_speed, dt) < 0.0:
        return False, speed, "motion-domain"
    accelerated = speed + thrust * dt
    damped = accelerated * max(0.0, 1.0 - drag * dt)
    bounded = min(damped, max_speed)
    if bounded > max_speed:
        return False, bounded, "speed-cap"
    assert 0.0 <= bounded <= max_speed
    return True, round(bounded, 3), "inertial-ship"
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Asteroids team handles case 7193 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to integrate rotation, thrust, inertia, drag, and speed limits with frame-rate-independent Vector2 motion; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Tune rotation, acceleration, drag, cap, input latency, and feedback against recorded player tasks and objective traces.

options.text: Keep the plausible Ship Feel Evidence output and hide the violated frame-rate-independent inertial ship controller assumption instead of explaining the failure. That claim fails the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary for Ship Feel Evidence.
